name: Agent Permissions
description: Permission and approval engine for OpenClaw agents. Any plugin can register per-tool resolvers; this engine walks rule sources (session/local/user/config), applies the three-bucket policy (allow/deny/ask), and surfaces in-chat approval via OpenClaw's requireApproval mechanism. Works with built-in tools (bash, file edit, etc.) and any plugin-registered tool. No network dependency.

defaultMode.description: Global session disposition. 'default' = ask on anything not allow-listed. 'bypassPermissions' = allow everything except hard denies. Mode layers on top of rules.

allow.description: Rule strings in the form 'ToolName' or 'ToolName(content)'. Content supports exact match, legacy prefix 'foo:*', and wildcard 'foo *'.

dangerousPatterns.description: Prefixes that may match allow rules but can never be allow-always-persisted. Defaults to the built-in list (python/node/eval/curl/wget/ssh/sudo/…). Override here to extend or replace.

userRulesPath.description: Defaults to ~/.openclaw/permissions.json. Persisted user-scope allow-always rules live here.

localRulesPath.description: Defaults to .openclaw/permissions.json in CWD. Persisted workspace-scope allow-always rules live here.

approvalTimeoutMs.description: How long to wait for an in-chat approval response (ms). Capped by the gateway.
